findings.message: Runtime deps flask>=3.0.0, portalocker>=2.8.0, pyyaml>=6.0.0 use open >= specifiers. uv.lock pins exact versions for managed installs but pipx/pip users without the lockfile may pull future breaking majors.
"A handful of dimensions" — vague quantifier (comparable to R01 'several') with no measurable criterion for count.
"a few lines: parse the threshold flag" — vague quantifier (comparable to R01 'some') with no measurable criterion.
EVO_TRACES_DIR env var determines where trace files are written; EVO_RESULT_PATH determines the result file path. By design (evo wire protocol); caller is evo run which controls these vars.
"without meaningfully different framings" — 'meaningfully' is a vague modifier with no operationalized criterion for when framing difference is sufficient.
"a meaningfully different idea after an evaluated/discarded outcome" — same vague modifier as optimize/SKILL.md L147; subagents cannot determine what 'meaningful' means without a threshold.

subprocess.run calls the claude CLI (network call to Anthropic API) with a prompt constructed from internal CASES data. No shell=True; inputs are not from untrusted external sources. Eval-tooling context only.
Writes raw LLM output to /tmp/rlm_narr_{case_id}_raw.txt on JSON parse failure. case_id is drawn from the hardcoded CASES dict so no traversal risk, but /tmp writes are non-portable on Windows.
subprocess.run calls the claude CLI with a hardcoded prompt string for trial execution. No shell=True; no user-controlled input in the command array.
Creates trial directory in tempfile.gettempdir() for test isolation. uuid4 suffix prevents collisions; intentional isolation pattern.
subprocess.run calls the claude CLI as an LLM judge with a prompt assembled from ground truth + model output JSON. No shell=True; data is local fixture content, not external user input.
"A handful of dimensions" — vague quantifier (comparable to R01 'several') with no measurable criterion for count.
"a few lines: parse the threshold flag" — vague quantifier (comparable to R01 'some') with no measurable criterion.
"without meaningfully different framings" — 'meaningfully' is a vague modifier with no operationalized criterion for when framing difference is sufficient.
"a meaningfully different idea after an evaluated/discarded outcome" — same vague modifier as optimize/SKILL.md L147; subagents cannot determine what 'meaningful' means without a threshold.

subprocess.run calls the claude CLI (network call to Anthropic API) with a prompt constructed from internal CASES data. No shell=True; inputs are not from untrusted external sources. Eval-tooling context only.
subprocess.run calls the claude CLI with a hardcoded prompt string for trial execution. No shell=True; no user-controlled input in the command array.
subprocess.run calls the claude CLI as an LLM judge with a prompt assembled from ground truth + model output JSON. No shell=True; data is local fixture content, not external user input.
Writes raw LLM output to /tmp/rlm_narr_{case_id}_raw.txt on JSON parse failure. case_id is drawn from the hardcoded CASES dict so no traversal risk, but /tmp writes are non-portable on Windows.
Creates trial directory in tempfile.gettempdir() for test isolation. uuid4 suffix prevents collisions; intentional isolation pattern.
EVO_TRACES_DIR env var determines where trace files are written; EVO_RESULT_PATH determines the result file path. By design (evo wire protocol); caller is evo run which controls these vars.
